docs.title: Welcome to Python DSA Notes 👋
docs.text: "Simple is better than complex. Complex is better than complicated." — Tim Peters (The Zen of Python)

docs.title: 📖 About This Documentation
docs.text:
This is a personal reference guide for Python Programming and Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA). It covers everything from the basics of Python syntax to advanced data structures and algorithms — written in a clean, beginner-friendly format with real examples and code snippets.

Whether you're just starting out or revising for interviews, this doc has you covered. 🚀

docs.title: 🔵 Basics
docs.text:
Core Python language fundamentals every programmer must know.

Topic Description Variables & Data Types int, float, str, bool, None and more Input & Output print, input, f-strings, format Control Flow if, elif, else, ternary expressions Loops for, while, break, continue, range Functions def, arguments, return, scope, lambda List Comprehensions Compact loops, filtering, nested comprehensions Error Handling try, except, finally, raising exceptions Modules & Imports import, from, as, standard library

docs.title: 📊 Complexity Analysis
docs.text:
Understanding how efficient your code is.

Topic Description Big O, Big Θ, Big Ω Asymptotic notations explained Time & Space Complexity Analyzing algorithm efficiency

docs.title: ⚙️ Algorithms
docs.text:
Step-by-step problem solving techniques.

Topic Description Sorting Bubble, Merge, Quick, Heap sort Searching Linear, Binary search Recursion & Backtracking Recursive thinking, N-Queens Dynamic Programming Memoization, tabulation, classic problems Greedy Algorithms Activity selection, Huffman coding Graph Algorithms Dijkstra, BFS, DFS, Kruskal, Prim

docs.title: 🗃️ Data Structures
docs.text:
Building blocks for efficient programs.

Topic Description Lists Dynamic arrays, slicing, operations Tuples Immutable sequences, packing, unpacking Sets Unique elements, set operations Dictionaries Key-value pairs, hash maps, methods Stack LIFO, push, pop, applications Queue FIFO, deque, priority queue Linked List Singly, doubly, circular Trees Binary tree, BST, traversals Heap Min/Max heap, heapq module Graphs Adjacency list/matrix, traversal

docs.title: 🏆 Practice Problems
docs.text:
Test your knowledge with curated problems.

Level Description Easy 🟢 Fundamentals, basic logic Medium 🟡 Moderate DSA problems Hard 🔴 Advanced, interview-level problems

docs.title: 🚀 How to Use This Doc
docs.text:
• Use the left sidebar to navigate between topics
  • Use the search bar (top right) to quickly find anything
  • Use the Table of Contents on the right to jump within a page
  • Code blocks have a copy button — use it!

docs.title: 🛠️ Tech Stack
docs.text:
• Language: Python
  • Docs: MkDocs
  • Theme: Material for MkDocs
  • Hosted on: GitHub Pages

docs.title: 📬 Source
docs.text:
Getting Started

New to Python? Start with Variables & Data Types and work your way through the Basics section before moving to DSA topics.
